design_philosophy.core_loop: Player selects character, fights sequence of opponents with increasing difficulty, unlocks boss characters through progression
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Executing combos and frame-perfect attacks against AI opponents with varying difficulty curves; mastering character matchups across a diverse roster
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a martial arts fighter and defeat increasingly powerful opponents, culminating in secret dream match against legendary crossover character
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Introduces combo system as core mechanic, deliberately makes four previous boss-only characters now playable (democratizing high-tier roster), features cross-franchise hidden boss (Ryo Sakazaki) that inspired creation of The King of Fighters series

difficulty.checkpointing: Single-player mode has player fight sequence of opponents; progression continues through wins
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can choose their first opponent from eight regular characters, suggesting some difficulty selection agency; completion of two-round wins triggers special dream match with Ryo

progression.unlock_mechanism: Four previously CPU-only boss characters (Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser) become playable upon game release; Ryo Sakazaki becomes playable in home versions and is accessible as hidden opponent in arcade single-player mode after winning all matches in two rounds
replayability_hooks: Character roster expanded to fifteen playable characters including newly unlocked bosses
Multiple character matchups provide varied playstyle experiences
Dream Match with Ryo serves as special endgame unlock condition
Versus multiplayer mode supports character mastery and competitive play

world_building_techniques: Character-specific stages with detailed, humorous backgrounds
Character roster includes legacy characters returning from original Fatal Fury and Art of Fighting (cross-franchise narrative connection)
Hidden boss character (Ryo Sakazaki) serves as meta-narrative reward, establishing universe-spanning story potential

boss_design.philosophy: Four boss characters from Fatal Fury 2 (Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser) made playable in Special, balancing arcade progression with home version accessibility; progression culminates in Geese Howard as penultimate boss and Ryo Sakazaki as special dream match opponent
notable_bosses: Billy Kane - previously AI-only, now playable
Axel Hawk - previously AI-only, now playable
Laurence Blood - previously AI-only, now playable
Wolfgang Krauser - previously AI-only, now playable
Ryo Sakazaki - cross-franchise secret boss, Art of Fighting protagonist

faction_indicators: Character roster drawn from international martial arts tournament context (characters from multiple nations competing)
Geese Howard represents antagonistic power structure (recurring villain archetype)
Ryo Sakazaki represents inter-franchise alliance/universe expansion
danger_signals: Boss characters designed with distinctive combat patterns requiring player adaptation
Increased opponent difficulty scale through single-player progression
Dream Match format positions Ryo as ultimate challenge/aspirational goal
environmental_storytelling: Stage backgrounds are character-specific, providing visual context for fighter origin
Detailed, humorous background elements suggest personality of each fighter's home location
Fighting across international locations (Italy mentioned in gameplay screenshot) establishes tournament world setting
